title: Sarga 73
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: virtue and right conduct, mortality and immortality. The passage moves from “Having sent off Shatrughna, Rama, accompanied by his two brothers, happily rejoiced while ruling the kingdom righteously.” through “The speaker asserts that he does not recall ever speaking an untruth, committing violence, or performing any sin against any living being.” through “I will die at the king's gate with my wife, like a helpless person, and then, O Rama, having incurred the sin of killing a Brahmin, you may be…”

Themes: mortality and immortality. The passage moves from “O King, accompanied by your brothers, you will obtain a long life; O greatly powerful one, we have lived happily in your kingdom until now.” through “Indeed, subjects not protected according to law perish due to the king's faults, and when the king is ill-behaved, people die untimely deaths.” through “Thus, repeatedly importuning the king with various kinds of words, the father, deeply distressed, clasps his son. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे…”

verses.sanskrit: प्रस्थाप्य तु स शत्रुघ्नं भ्रातृभ्यां सह राघवः । प्रमुमोद सुखी राज्यं धर्मेण परिपालयन् ।। 7.73.1 ।।
verses.translation: Having sent off Shatrughna, Rama, accompanied by his two brothers, happily rejoiced while ruling the kingdom righteously.

verses.sanskrit: ततः कतिपयाहःसु वृद्धो जानपदो द्विजः । मृतं बालमुपादाय राजद्वारमुपागमत् ।। 7.73.2 ।।
verses.translation: Then, after a few days, an old Brahmin citizen, holding his dead child, approached the royal gate.

verses.sanskrit: रुदन्बहुविधा वाचः स्नेहदुःखसमन्विताः । असकृत्पुत्र पुत्रेति वाक्यमेतदुवाच ह ।। 7.73.3 ।।
verses.translation: Weeping, he uttered various words filled with affection and sorrow, repeatedly exclaiming, "O son, O son!"

verses.sanskrit: किं नु मे दुष्कृतं कर्म पुरा देहान्तरे कृतम् । यदहं पुत्रमेकं त्वां पश्यामि निधनं गतम् ।। 7.73.4 ।।
verses.translation: He lamented, "What evil deed did I commit in a previous birth that I now see you, my only son, having met your death?"

verses.sanskrit: अप्राप्तयौवनं बालं पञ्चवर्षसहस्रकम् । अकाले कालमापन्नं मम दुःखाय पुत्रक ।। 7.73.5 ।।
verses.translation: The child, though five thousand years old, had not yet reached youth and met an untimely death, causing great sorrow to the speaker.

verses.sanskrit: अल्पैरहोभिर्निधनं गमिष्यामि न संशयः । अहं च जननी चैव तव शोकेन पुत्रक ।। 7.73.6 ।।
verses.translation: The speaker and the mother will undoubtedly die within a few days due to the grief over the son's death.

verses.sanskrit: न स्मराम्यनृतं ह्युक्तं न च हिंसां स्मराम्यहम् । सर्वेषां प्राणिनां पापं कृतं नैव स्मराम्यहम् ।। 7.73.7 ।।
verses.translation: The speaker asserts that he does not recall ever speaking an untruth, committing violence, or performing any sin against any living being.

verses.sanskrit: केनाद्य दुष्कृतेनायं बाल एव ममात्मजः । अकृत्वा पितृकार्याणि गतो वैवस्वतक्षयम् ।। 7.73.8 ।।
verses.translation: The speaker wonders what sin he committed that his son, still a child, has died without fulfilling his duties to his ancestors.

verses.sanskrit: नेदृशं दृष्टपूर्वं मे श्रुतं वा घोरदर्शनम् । मृत्युरप्राप्तकालानां रामस्य विषये यथा ।। 7.73.9 ।।
verses.translation: The speaker states that he has never before seen or heard of such a terrible sight as the untimely death of those in Rama's kingdom.

verses.sanskrit: रामस्य दुष्कृतं किञ्चिन्महदस्ति न संशयः । यथा हि विषयस्थानां बालानां मृत्युरागतः ।। 7.73.10 ।।
verses.translation: There is no doubt that Rama has committed some great sin, just as death has come upon the children living in his kingdom.

verses.sanskrit: नह्यन्यविषयस्थानां बालानां मृत्युतो भयम् । त्वं राजञ्जीवयस्वैनं बालं मृत्युवशं गतम् ।। 7.73.11 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, children in other kingdoms do not fear death, so O King, you must revive this child who has fallen under the sway of death.

verses.sanskrit: राजद्वारि मरिष्यामि पत्न्या सार्धमनाथवत् । ब्रह्महत्यां ततो राम समुपेत्य सुखी भव ।। 7.73.12 ।।
verses.translation: I will die at the king's gate with my wife, like a helpless person, and then, O Rama, having incurred the sin of killing a Brahmin, you may be happy.

verses.sanskrit: भ्रातृभिः सहितो राजन्दीर्घमायुरवाप्स्यसि । उषिताः स्म सुखं राज्ये तवास्मिन्सुमहाबल ।। 7.73.13 ।।
verses.translation: O King, accompanied by your brothers, you will obtain a long life; O greatly powerful one, we have lived happily in your kingdom until now.

verses.sanskrit: इदं तु पतितं तस्मात्तव राम वशे स्थिताः । कालस्य वशमापन्नाः स्वल्पं हि नहि नः सुखम् ।। 7.73.14 ।।
verses.translation: But this misfortune has now befallen us, O Rama, who are under your control, as we have fallen under the sway of time, and indeed, our happiness is now very little.

verses.sanskrit: सम्प्रत्यनाथो विषय इक्ष्वाकूणां महात्मनाम् । रामं नाथमिहासाद्य बालान्तकरणं ध्रुवम् ।। 7.73.15 ।।
verses.translation: Now, this kingdom of the great Ikshvakus, having obtained Rama as its protector, is certainly causing the death of children.

verses.sanskrit: राजदोषैर्विपद्यन्ते प्रजा ह्यविधिपालिताः । असद्वृत्ते तु नृपतावकाले म्रियते जनः ।। 7.73.16 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, subjects not protected according to law perish due to the king's faults, and when the king is ill-behaved, people die untimely deaths.

verses.sanskrit: यद्वा पुरेष्वयुक्तानि जना जनपदेषु च । कुर्वते न च रक्षा ऽस्ति तदा कालकृतं भयम् ।। 7.73.17 ।।
verses.translation: Or when people commit improper acts in cities and districts, and there is no protection, then there is fear caused by the times.

verses.sanskrit: सुव्यक्तं राजदोषो हि भविष्यति न संशयः । पुरे जनपदे चापि ततो बालवधो ह्ययम् ।। 7.73.18 ।।
verses.translation: It is very clear that there will be no doubt of a king's fault in the city and the district, and therefore, this child-killing is indeed due to that.

verses.sanskrit: एवं बहुविधैर्वाक्यैरुपरुध्य मुहुर्मुहुः । राजानं दुःखसन्तप्तः सुतं तमुपगूहते ।। 7.73.19 ।।
verses.translation: Thus, repeatedly importuning the king with various kinds of words, the father, deeply distressed, clasps his son. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे त्रिसप्ततितमः सर्गः ।। 73 ।। Thus ends the seventy-third sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
